purpose: Composed conformance profile: admission (signer independence) + recompute (verdict = f(controls)) + chain-fork (head_hash = sha256(content_hash|prev_head_hash)). Requested by rpelevin on autogen#7353 (2026-07-03).

vectors.note: Signer is the actor's own payment wallet -- self-approval, not an independent verdict. Fails closed on WHO signed it; the hash and recorded verdict are otherwise valid.

controls.policy=VIOLATION implies f(controls)=DENY, but the recorded verdict is ALLOW. Signer is a legitimate independent policy-issuer -- fails on WHETHER the verdict follows from its own cited inputs, not on WHO signed it.
The accepted decision committed as a receipt chain entry. Recomputing head_hash from the identical content + prior head reproduces the identical digest, deterministically.

A divergent entry at the SAME sequence position (same prior_head, different content). Produces a DIFFERENT head_hash from axis3-chain-entry-a -- both heads independently computable, so a fork is a detectable conflict, not a silent overwrite.

independent_verification.result: byte-identical head_hash values on both chain-entry vectors
independent_verification.method: This repo's verify.py was written independently from the construction description (content_hash + head_hash formula), not copied from any third party's code, then cross-checked against babyblueviper1's independently-published head_hash values. Both computations used the same source fixture (presidio-x402-decision-ref-v1.fixture.json, originally contributed to this repo by presidio-v), which is verified byte-identical between both repos.
